mapping.a_element: tokens drawn from a fixed vocabulary (one-dimensional token sequence)
mapping.b_element: notes/symbols drawn from a limited alphabet (one-dimensional melodic sequence)

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic substrate of one-dimensional sequences over a finite alphabet; the actual machinery differs entirely. Paper A's mechanism is BPE vocabulary construction (continuing greedy merge operations) plus neural embedding initialization and continued pre-training, whereas Paper B's mechanism is rhythm-aware sequence alignment and evolutionary statistics (substitution matrices, positional conservation, covariance) — there is no BPE-style merging or embedding adaptation in B and no alignment or evolutionary-rate estimation in A, so no role-for-role mapping of the driving processes holds.
